items.title: Use always-on cloud agents for review, triage, and incident chores
items.why: Background automations keep review and maintenance work moving without waiting for someone to open the IDE.
how_to_steps: Create a new automation from the Agents Window, cursor.com/automations, or the /automate skill.
Pick a trigger such as pull request opened, CI completed, Slack, webhook, or cron.
Write the prompt and enable only the tools it needs, such as PR comments, Slack, MCP, or memories.
Choose whether it should run against one repo, multiple repos, or no repo, then save and activate it.
items.evidence: Cursor says Bugbot-like automations run thousands of times a day and have caught millions of bugs; its internal security-review automation has caught multiple vulnerabilities and critical bugs; Runlayer says the setup helps them move faster than teams five times their size.
items.try_monday: Ship one cron or PR-open automation that reviews merged code for missing tests and posts only high-confidence findings.

items.title: Add Codex CLI as a terminal-first coding agent
items.why: A local terminal agent gives you long-running code work and PR review loops without being tied to a single editor.
how_to_steps: Install Codex CLI with the Mac or Linux install script, npm, Homebrew, or a release binary.
Run codex and sign in with ChatGPT so it uses the plan you already pay for.
Start code tasks from the terminal and choose an approval mode that matches your risk tolerance.
Escalate longer jobs to Codex cloud or GitHub review flows when you want work to keep running after you close the terminal.
items.evidence: OpenAI says GPT-5-Codex can work for more than 7 hours on complex tasks, cached cloud tasks cut median completion time by 90%, bottom-decile turns use 93.7% fewer tokens than GPT-5, and Codex reviews the vast majority of internal PRs while catching hundreds of issues per day.
items.try_monday: Install Codex CLI on one workstation and compare one feature branch done with terminal-agent help versus the current manual loop.
